# AN-064: TMC4671 Linear Motor Control

**Type:** Knowledge
**Summary:** Trinamic AN-064 — FOC-based linear motor control using TMC4671, covering pole pair configuration, ABN encoder setup (PPR calculation), linear scaling factors, register-level configuration, and TMCL-IDE wizard workflow.
**Tags:** #tmc #tmc4671 #linear-motor #foc #encoder #application-note #trinamic
**Status:** draft
**Updated:** 2026-04-08
**Source:** auto-generated by opencode/claude-opus-4.6
**Related:** [[AN-003-dcStep]], [[TMC4671-LA]], [[tmc-4671]], [[TMC-Driver-Comparison]]

---

## Overview

**Author:** Bernhard Dwersteg (Trinamic / Analog Devices).
**Source PDF:** https://www.analog.com/media/en/technical-documentation/app-notes/an-064.pdf

AN-064 describes Field-Oriented Control (FOC) of three-phase linear motors using the TMC4671 hardware servo controller. The TMC4671 implements all FOC mathematics in hardware (Clarke/Park transforms, PI current loops, space-vector PWM), freeing the host MCU from any real-time control tasks. AN-064 maps the TMC4671 rotary-motor register model onto the linear-motor domain — the sole structural difference is forcing `N_POLE_PAIRS = 1` and expressing spatial quantities in µm rather than electrical degrees.

**Operating modes covered:** torque mode, velocity mode, position mode (closed-loop via ABN encoder).

**Evaluation hardware:**
- TMC4671-EVAL — main FOC controller board (SPI host interface, USB-to-UART for TMCL-IDE)
- TMC6100-EVAL — 3-phase gate driver board; daisy-chained via SPI to TMC4671-EVAL; drives motor phases

**Signal chain:** Host MCU → TMC4671 (SPI) → TMC6100 (SPI, gate drive) → linear motor phases; ABN encoder → TMC4671 encoder inputs.

---

## Linear Motor vs. Rotary Motor Configuration

A rotary BLDC with *N* physical pole pairs produces *N* electrical cycles per mechanical revolution. The TMC4671 FOC engine uses `N_POLE_PAIRS` to convert between electrical angle and mechanical position.

For a linear motor the equivalent spatial period is the **pole pair length** — the distance corresponding to one full electrical cycle (pole pitch × 2, measured in µm). Because this length already defines the spatial-to-electrical relationship, the TMC4671 must be told there is exactly one pole pair; the actual physical scaling is absorbed into the ABN encoder PPR setting.

| Parameter | Rotary BLDC | Linear motor |
|---|---|---|
| `MOTOR_TYPE` | 3 (3-phase BLDC) | 3 (identical) |
| `N_POLE_PAIRS` | actual count (e.g., 4) | **always 1** |
| One electrical cycle | 1/N_POLE_PAIRS revolution | 1 × pole_pair_length [µm] |
| Position unit | encoder counts / revolution | µm (via pole_pair_length scaling) |
| Velocity unit | counts/s or RPM | µm/s |
| PPR definition | encoder lines per revolution | encoder counts per pole-pair length |

Setting `N_POLE_PAIRS = 1` makes the FOC engine treat one full encoder count cycle (0 → ABN_DECODER_PPR − 1) as one complete electrical period. The concept of "revolution" becomes: traverse one pole-pair length = one virtual revolution. This re-uses the rotary firmware unchanged.

---

## Register Configuration

All TMC4671 registers are 32-bit wide, accessed over SPI (address phase + data phase). Full address space: 0x00–0x7F.

| Register | Address | Value / Setting | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `MOTOR_TYPE_N_POLE_PAIRS` | 0x1B | `MOTOR_TYPE=3`, `N_POLE_PAIRS=1` | 3-phase BLDC mode; linear forces pole pairs = 1 |
| `PWM_POLARITIES` | 0x17 | per gate driver | High-side / low-side polarity matching TMC6100 |
| `PWM_MAXCNT` | 0x18 | e.g., 3999 | Defines PWM period: f_PWM = f_CLK / (2 × MAXCNT); 3999 → 25 kHz at 200 MHz |
| `ADC_I0_SCALE_OFFSET` | 0x24 | calibrated | Phase current channel 0 scale + zero-offset |
| `ADC_I1_SCALE_OFFSET` | 0x24 | calibrated | Phase current channel 1 scale + zero-offset |
| `ABN_DECODER_MODE` | 0x25 | 0x00000000 | ABN edge counting mode, polarity, N-channel config |
| `ABN_DECODER_PPR` | 0x26 | pole_pair_length / encoder_res | Counts per virtual revolution = one pole-pair length |
| `ABN_DECODER_COUNT` | 0x27 | 0 (after init) | Current encoder count; writable for initialization |
| `ABN_DECODER_PHI_E` | 0x29 | read-only | Electrical angle derived from encoder count |
| `PHI_E_SELECTION` | 0x52 | 3 | Route commutation angle from phi_e_abn |
| `VELOCITY_SELECTION` | 0x50 | 3 | Route velocity feedback from phi_e_abn |
| `POSITION_SELECTION` | 0x51 | 3 | Route position feedback from phi_e_abn |
| `MODE_RAMP_MODE_MOTION` | 0x63 | 1 / 2 / 3 | Active motion mode: 1=torque, 2=velocity, 3=position |
| `PID_TORQUE_FLUX_TARGET` | 0x64 | signed 16 + 16 bit | Torque/flux setpoint in torque mode |
| `PID_VELOCITY_TARGET` | 0x65 | signed 32-bit (µm/s) | Velocity setpoint in velocity mode |
| `PID_POSITION_TARGET` | 0x68 | signed 32-bit (µm) | Position setpoint in position mode |
| `PID_POSITION_ACTUAL` | 0x6B | read-only | Actual position in µm (after scaling) |
| `PID_TORQUE_P` / `_I` | 0x56 / 0x57 | tuned | Torque loop PI gains |
| `PID_VELOCITY_P` / `_I` | 0x58 / 0x59 | tuned | Velocity loop PI gains |
| `PID_POSITION_P` | 0x5A | tuned | Position loop P gain (integral typically 0 for linear stages) |

> **Note:** Register addresses follow TMC4671 datasheet rev 1.07. ADC channel registers use a bank-select mechanism — see full datasheet for bank-select write sequence before programming ADC scale/offset.

---

## ABN Encoder Configuration

### PPR Calculation

The `ABN_DECODER_PPR` register defines how many encoder pulses correspond to one virtual revolution (= one pole-pair length traversal). Core formula:

```
ABN_DECODER_PPR = pole_pair_length [µm] / encoder_resolution [µm/count]
```

**Example from AN-064:**
- Pole pair length: 30,000 µm (30 mm)
- Encoder resolution: 1 µm/count (1 µm line pitch × 4-edge decoding? No — 1 µm is the decoded count spacing)
- Result: `ABN_DECODER_PPR = 30,000 / 1 = 30,000`

**Higher-resolution example:**
- Pole pair length: 20,000 µm (20 mm)
- Encoder resolution: 0.5 µm/count
- Result: `ABN_DECODER_PPR = 40,000`

**Hardware limit:** `ABN_DECODER_PPR` is a 24-bit unsigned field → maximum value = 2^24 − 1 = **16,777,215**. If your pole_pair_length / encoder_resolution ratio exceeds this, either use a coarser encoder or apply a hardware pre-scaler.

### Initialization Mode 0 — Open-Loop Forced Alignment

Incremental ABN encoders have no absolute position reference. On each power cycle the commutation angle must be established:

1. Set `PHI_E_SELECTION = 2` (phi_e_ext = externally forced angle)
2. Apply a defined DC flux current at 0° electrical angle via `PID_TORQUE_FLUX_TARGET` (flux component only, torque = 0)
3. Motor mover locks to the nearest d-axis alignment position; wait for settling (typical: 200–500 ms)
4. Write 0 to `ABN_DECODER_COUNT` (register 0x27) to zero the count at the known mechanical alignment
5. Set `PHI_E_SELECTION = 3` (phi_e_abn) — closed-loop commutation now valid from encoder
6. Enable velocity or position mode

Re-initialization required on each power cycle unless an absolute reference (limit switch + known offset, or N-channel index pulse) restores position.

### ABN_DECODER_MODE Bit Fields (register 0x25)

| Bit | Name | 0 | 1 |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | `n_channel_invert` | N active high | N active low |
| 4 | `use_abn_as_n` | Normal A/B/N | Use A/B edges to generate synthetic N |
| 8 | `ab_swap` | A=A, B=B | A/B signals swapped |
| 12 | `disable_filter` | Digital glitch filter on | Filter disabled |

Setting `ab_swap = 1` reverses the count direction; equivalent to swapping two motor phases but done in firmware. Use this to match positive encoder count direction to the desired positive motor direction without rewiring.

---

## Linear Scaling Factor

The `pole_pair_length` parameter (entered in µm in the TMCL-IDE wizard) creates a direct 1:1 mapping between register values and physical distance. Once configured:

| Quantity | Unit |
|---|---|
| 1 position register count | 1 µm linear travel |
| `PID_POSITION_TARGET` value | µm from reference |
| `PID_VELOCITY_TARGET` value | µm/s |
| `PID_POSITION_ACTUAL` readback | µm |

This eliminates host-side unit conversion entirely. A target of `PID_POSITION_TARGET = 50000` commands 50 mm of travel.

**Why it works:** With `ABN_DECODER_PPR = pole_pair_length / encoder_resolution` and `N_POLE_PAIRS = 1`, each encoder count advances the TMC4671 internal position counter by exactly 1. If encoder_resolution = 1 µm, each count = 1 µm. The TMCL-IDE wizard stores the `pole_pair_length` value and uses it to pre-scale the display and target registers consistently.

---

## Feedback Selection

Three independent multiplexers in the TMC4671 route feedback signals to the control loops. AN-064 sets all three to `phi_e_abn` (value 3):

| Register | Address | Value | Source routed |
|---|---|---|---|
| `PHI_E_SELECTION` | 0x52 | 3 | phi_e_abn — commutation angle from encoder |
| `VELOCITY_SELECTION` | 0x50 | 3 | phi_e_abn — velocity from encoder count rate |
| `POSITION_SELECTION` | 0x51 | 3 | phi_e_abn — position from cumulative encoder count |

**All supported phi_e sources (TMC4671 datasheet):**

| Value | Source | Typical use |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `phi_e_ext` | Open-loop; forced electrical angle (used during init) |
| 2 | `phi_e_ext` (hall input mapped) | Hall sensor commutation |
| 3 | `phi_e_abn` | ABN encoder — this application note |
| 4 | `phi_e_hall` | Hall sensor (dedicated hall decoder) |
| 5 | `phi_e_biquad_abn` | Filtered ABN (biquad IIR; for high-noise environments) |

> **Critical:** All three selection registers must point to the same physical feedback source. Mismatching (e.g., hall for PHI_E but ABN for POSITION) introduces a phase offset between commutation angle and actual position, causing torque ripple or instability.

---

## TMCL-IDE Wizard Workflow

The TMCL-IDE "Motor Configuration Wizard" automates the full register sequence. AN-064 documents seven steps:

**Step 1 — Motor Type and Pole Pairs**
- Motor class: BLDC (3-phase)
- Number of pole pairs: **1** — mandatory for any linear motor
- Commutation: FOC

**Step 2 — ADC Configuration**
- Wizard drives a zero-current condition and measures ADC offsets for both current sense channels
- Writes calibrated values to `ADC_I0_SCALE_OFFSET` and `ADC_I1_SCALE_OFFSET`
- Prerequisite: TMC6100 gate driver powered; shunt resistors installed on EVAL board

**Step 3 — Encoder Configuration**
- Interface: ABN (quadrature incremental)
- Enter pole_pair_length and encoder_resolution; wizard computes and writes `ABN_DECODER_PPR`
- Set direction: verify positive encoder count = positive motion direction (swap AB if needed)
- Run encoder init (Mode 0 forced alignment): wizard applies alignment current, waits for settling, zeros count

**Step 4 — Feedback Selection**
- All three dropdowns set to `phi_e_abn` (value 3)
- Wizard writes: `VELOCITY_SELECTION = 3`, `POSITION_SELECTION = 3`, `PHI_E_SELECTION = 3`

**Step 5 — Linear Scaling Factor**
- Enter `pole_pair_length` in µm
- Wizard programs the scaling and confirms that position/velocity register units are now µm / µm/s

**Step 6 — PI Loop Tuning**
- **Torque/flux loops** (innermost): Start Kp ≈ 100, Ki ≈ 1000 (TMC4671 normalized scale); increase Kp until step response is crisp; watch for current oscillation at high Kp
- **Velocity loop** (middle): Start Kp ≈ 50, Ki ≈ 100; step-test 10% of max velocity; adjust until overshoot < 5% and no steady-state error
- **Position loop** (outermost): Typically P-only (Ki = 0) for linear stages; tune Kp for desired settling time vs. overshoot tradeoff

**Step 7 — Functional Test**
- Set `MODE_RAMP_MODE_MOTION = 3` (position mode)
- Write test target to `PID_POSITION_TARGET` (e.g., 10,000 for 10 mm)
- Read back `PID_POSITION_ACTUAL`; verify tracking; observe current and velocity waveforms in TMCL-IDE oscilloscope view

---

## Hardware Setup

### Evaluation Board Stack

```
Host PC ──USB── TMCL-IDE ──USB/SPI bridge──
                                            |
                                    TMC4671-EVAL
                                    (FOC engine, SPI target)
                                            | SPI daisy-chain
                                    TMC6100-EVAL
                                    (3-phase gate driver)
                                            | U / V / W phases
                                    Linear motor coils
                                            
TMC4671-EVAL encoder header ←── A / B / N ── Linear encoder strip
```

### Connection Summary

| Signal | From | To | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| SPI CLK / SDI / SDO / CS | Host or USB bridge | TMC4671-EVAL | 3.3V; SPI mode 3; max ~4 MHz on EVAL |
| SPI daisy-chain | TMC4671-EVAL | TMC6100-EVAL | TMC6100 SPI slave for gate commands |
| Motor phases U, V, W | TMC6100-EVAL | Linear motor | Phase order determines positive travel direction |
| ABN A / B / N | Linear encoder | TMC4671-EVAL header | 5V or 3.3V; check EVAL level-shifter jumper |
| Current sense (shunt) | On-board shunts | TMC4671 ADC inputs | Calibrated in wizard Step 2 |
| VM | Bench PSU | TMC6100-EVAL VM | Per motor spec; TMC6100 abs max = 60 V |
| GND | Common | All boards | Star GND from PSU recommended |

### Phase Order Verification

After initialization, command a small positive velocity (e.g., 500 µm/s). If the stage moves in the negative direction, correct by **either**:
- Swap any two motor phase wires (U↔V, V↔W, or U↔W), **or**
- Set `ABN_DECODER_MODE` bit 8 (`ab_swap = 1`) to flip encoder direction

Never do both simultaneously — they cancel each other.

---

## Key Formulas

| Quantity | Formula |
|---|---|
| `ABN_DECODER_PPR` | `pole_pair_length [µm] / encoder_resolution [µm/count]` |
| Maximum PPR | `2^24 − 1 = 16,777,215` |
| Electrical angle | `phi_e = (ABN_COUNT / ABN_PPR) × 65536` (TMC4671 internal 16-bit angle units) |
| Position (after scaling) | 1 register count = 1 µm |
| Velocity (after scaling) | 1 register count = 1 µm/s |
| PWM frequency | `f_CLK / (2 × PWM_MAXCNT)` |
